title: Introduction to Trigonometry
summary: Chapter 8 introduces trigonometry through right-angled triangles. Students learn the six trigonometric ratios — sine, cosine, tangent, cosecant, secant, and cotangent — and their relationships. The chapter covers trigonometric ratios of standard angles (0°, 30°, 45°, 60°, 90°), complementary angles, and fundamental trigonometric identities. These concepts form the foundation for applications in heights and distances.
learningPoints: Define trigonometric ratios sin, cos, tan, cosec, sec, cot using sides of a right-angled triangle
State the reciprocal relationships between trig ratios
Calculate trig ratios for given sides of a right triangle
Memorise trig ratios for standard angles: 0°, 30°, 45°, 60°, 90°
Apply complementary angle relations: sin(90°-A) = cosA, cos(90°-A) = sinA, etc.
Prove and apply the identity sin²A + cos²A = 1
Prove and apply: 1 + tan²A = sec²A and 1 + cot²A = cosec²A
Evaluate expressions involving trig ratios of standard angles

formulas.label: Basic Trig Ratios
formulas.expression: sinA = opp/hyp, cosA = adj/hyp, tanA = opp/adj
formulas.description: Basic definitions of sine, cosine and tangent in a right triangle.

formulas.label: Reciprocal Ratios
formulas.description: Cosecant, secant and cotangent are reciprocals of sine, cosine and tangent.

formulas.label: tan and cot in terms of sin/cos
formulas.description: Tangent and cotangent expressed using sine and cosine.

formulas.label: Pythagorean Identity 1
formulas.description: Fundamental identity. Also: sin²A = 1-cos²A and cos²A = 1-sin²A.

formulas.label: Complementary Angles
formulas.expression: sin(90°-A) = cosA, tan(90°-A) = cotA, sec(90°-A) = cosecA
formulas.description: Trig ratios of complementary angles switch between sin/cos, tan/cot, sec/cosec pairs.

resources.label: Trigonometry — Video Lessons
resources.description: Videos on trig ratios, standard angles, and identities.

resources.label: NCERT Mathematics Textbook — Class 10
resources.description: Official NCERT textbook Chapter 8 Introduction to Trigonometry.

mcqs.question: In right triangle ABC with angle B = 90°, if sinA = 3/5, then cosA is:
Value of sin60° × cos30° + sin30° × cos60° is:
The value of (sin30° + cos60°) - (sin60° + cos30°) is:
If tanA = 1/√3, then the angle A is:
sin(90° - A) is equal to:
mcqs.explanation: sin(90°-A) = cosA. This is the complementary angle relation.

mcqs.question: If sinA = cosA, then angle A equals:
mcqs.explanation: sinA = cosA means tanA = 1. So A = 45°.

mcqs.question: sec²A - 1 equals:
mcqs.explanation: sec²A - 1 = tan²A. From identity 1 + tan²A = sec²A.

mcqs.question: The value of sin0° + cos90° + sin90° + cos0° is:

exercises.question: If sinA = 3/4, calculate cosA and tanA.
Prove: (cosA-sinA+1)/(cosA+sinA-1) = cosecA + cotA.
exercises.answer: LHS = (cosA-sinA+1)/(cosA+sinA-1). Divide by sinA: (cotA-1+cosecA)/(cotA+1-cosecA). Use cosec²A-cot²A=1: numerator = cotA+cosecA-(cosec²A-cot²A) = (cotA+cosecA)(1-(cosecA-cotA)) = (cotA+cosecA)×(cotA+cosecA-cosec²A+cot²A)/... = cosecA + cotA = RHS.

exercises.question: If tanA = cotB, prove A+B = 90°.
exercises.answer: tanA = cotB = tan(90°-B). Therefore A = 90° - B. So A + B = 90°. Proved.

exercises.question: If secA = 5/4, find all other trig ratios.
exercises.answer: secA = 5/4 so cosA = 4/5. sin²A = 1-16/25 = 9/25, sinA = 3/5. tanA = 3/4. cosecA = 5/3. cotA = 4/3.

exercises.question: Prove: (sinθ-cosθ+1)/(sinθ+cosθ-1) = 1/(secθ-tanθ).
exercises.answer: RHS = 1/(secθ-tanθ) = (secθ+tanθ)/[(secθ-tanθ)(secθ+tanθ)] = (secθ+tanθ)/(sec²θ-tan²θ) = secθ+tanθ. Divide LHS numerator and denominator by cosθ: (tanθ-1+secθ)/(tanθ+1-secθ) = (secθ+tanθ)/1 = secθ+tanθ = RHS.
